vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on /var/log/messages File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /var/log/messages, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0600 /var/log/messages

triage_decision.reason: Changing the mode of /var/log/messages to a more restrictive setting (e.g., 0600) only affects log file readability and does not impact authentication, networking, or critical services. The remediation is straightforward, reversible, and aligns with standard STIG file permission hardening.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential loss of read access for non‑root processes that may rely on the log; Incorrect command syntax could leave the file with unintended permissions
